message:
Fix mpsc cancellation baton loss, pool FIFO after health check, and notify early-removal

mpsc.rs — Reserve cancellation baton passing:
- When Reserve is cancelled (checkpoint fails), the waiter was simply
  abandoned in the send_wakers queue. If this waiter was the head-of-line
  blocker and capacity was available, no other sender would be woken.
  Now dequeues the waiter on cancellation and, if it was head-of-line
  and capacity is available, wakes the next sender via take_next_sender_waker().
- When Receiver is dropped and Reserve detects disconnection, clears
  waiter_id since the receiver's drop already cleaned up the queue.
- Receiver::poll_recv now clears recv_waker on cancellation to prevent
  the stale waker from being stored after the future is dropped.

notify.rs — Revert over-eager baton passing on early removal:
- The previous commit added baton-passing in the two early-return paths
  where a waiter is removed because the generation changed. However,
  generation changes mean notify_all() was called, which already wakes
  ALL waiters — there is no baton to pass. The extra pass_baton() call
  was causing spurious wakeups and could double-notify.
- Reverted both paths back to simple remove + drop(waiters).
- The Drop impl still correctly handles the cancellation case where
  a notify_one() notification must be forwarded to the next waiter.

pool.rs — FIFO fairness after failed health check:
- When try_get_idle returns a resource that fails the health check,
  the waiter was removed from the queue but never re-registered,
  causing it to be silently dropped from the wait list. Now
  re-registers at the FRONT of the waiters deque to preserve FIFO order.
- Moved waiter cleanup (removing from return_wakers) to after the
  health check loop, so the waiter ID remains valid during retries.

tests/notify_bug.rs:
- Updated assertions: polling a notified future consumes the notification
  (Ready), while dropping a notified future passes the baton (the next
  future sees Ready). Split the compound assert_eq into two separate
  asserts with clearer messages.

Cargo.toml:
- Added tokio 1.49.0 dependency for async test infrastructure.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
